activities_projects.title: Critical Analysis of the Theme
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the central theme and moral of the poem
steps: Read the poem carefully and identify its main themes.
Discuss themes such as generosity, justice, and misuse of power.
Explain how the actions of Gopi and the Zamindar represent contrasting values.
Write a detailed paragraph explaining the main moral of the poem.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical thinking by analysing literary themes.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of interpretation and supporting arguments (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Character Comparison Activity
activities_projects.objective: Compare and contrast the characters of Gopi and the Zamindar
steps: Create a comparison table for Gopi and the Zamindar.
List their actions, motivations, and attitudes.
Provide examples from the poem supporting each point.
Write a concluding paragraph explaining how the contrast strengthens the story.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn comparative analysis of literary characters.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of comparison and textual evidence (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Symbolism Interpretation
activities_projects.objective: Interpret symbolic meanings in the poem
steps: Identify symbols such as the magic brush, the fortress, and the river.
Discuss what each symbol represents in the poem.
Explain how these symbols contribute to the message of the story.
Write a short analytical explanation for each symbol.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand symbolism and deeper literary meanings.
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of interpretation and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Alternative Ending Writing
activities_projects.objective: Encourage creative thinking and narrative skills
steps: Imagine a different ending to the poem.
Consider what might happen if Gopi chose to help the Zamindar.
Write a short alternative ending explaining the consequences.
Share and discuss your ending with classmates.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop creative writing and narrative reasoning.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, coherence, and moral reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Poetic Devices Identification
activities_projects.objective: Recognise poetic techniques used in the poem
steps: Read the poem carefully.
Identify poetic devices such as rhyme scheme, imagery, and repetition.
Write examples of these devices from the poem.
Explain how these devices enhance the poem’s meaning.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand literary techniques used in poetry.

activities_projects.title: Ethical Debate Activity
steps: Divide the class into two groups.
One group argues that Gopi should have helped the Zamindar.
The other group argues that Gopi was right to refuse.
Conduct a structured debate and summarise the strongest arguments.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop reasoning, argumentation, and ethical thinking.
activities_projects.evaluation: Strength of arguments and participation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Narrative Perspective Activity
steps: Rewrite a scene from the poem from the perspective of the Zamindar.
Describe how he feels when Gopi refuses his order.
Explain his thoughts during the chase scene.
Compare how the story changes when told from his viewpoint.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand narrative perspective and character motivation.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and understanding of viewpoint (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Social Message Reflection
activities_projects.objective: Connect literature with real-life social values
steps: Identify the social message conveyed by the poem.
Discuss how power and wealth are portrayed in the story.
Relate the poem’s message to real-world situations.
Write a reflective essay on the importance of using power responsibly.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect literature with social and ethical issues.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of reflection and clarity of ideas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Illustration Project
steps: Choose an important scene from the poem.
Create an illustration representing the scene.
Write a short explanation describing the significance of the scene.
Present your artwork to the class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students interpret literary scenes creatively through art.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and explanation of symbolism (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Modern-Day Magic Brush Essay
activities_projects.objective: Apply the story’s ideas to modern contexts
steps: Imagine a magic brush existed in today’s world.
Discuss how it could solve modern problems like poverty or pollution.
Write a detailed essay explaining how the brush could benefit society.
Conclude with the responsibilities that come with such power.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students apply literary ideas to contemporary issues.
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical reasoning, creativity, and clarity of writing (10 marks)
